findings.evidence: `Kernel.lean:59-63` uses `Fin n`; Mathlib's corresponding finite quadratic-form theorem is polymorphic, `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PosDef.lean:297: theorem posSemidef_iff_dotProduct_mulVec {M : Matrix n n R}` under `variable [Fintype n]`.
findings.fix: State it as `∀ {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι], (v : ι → α) → (x : ι → 𝕜) → 0 ≤ ∑ i, ∑ j, conj (x i) * x j * K (v i) (v j)` and derive the `Fin n` case if desired.
findings.issue: `kernel_sum_conj_mul_mul_nonneg` is under-generalized to `(n : ℕ) (v : Fin n → α) (x : Fin n → 𝕜)`, forcing users with an arbitrary finite index type to reindex through an equivalence.

findings.evidence: `Kernel.lean:77` quantifies only `∀ (n : ℕ) (v : Fin n → α)` even though `Matrix.PosSemidef` itself is defined by arbitrary finitely supported coefficients at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PosDef.lean:59-60`.
findings.fix: Quantify over arbitrary finite index types in the right-hand side, e.g. `∀ {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] (v : ι → α) (x : ι → 𝕜), ...`; the reverse direction can then use that finite type directly instead of converting `x.support` through `Fintype.equivFin`.
findings.issue: `posSemidef_kernel_iff` bakes the same `Fin n` specialization into the main characterization, making the introduction rule weaker than the natural finite-family statement.

summary: The finite-family API is specialized to `Fin n`, while the surrounding Mathlib `PosSemidef` API is naturally polymorphic over arbitrary finite index types. Generalize the kernel quadratic-form statements before adding this as reusable API.
